p.strong: Ring Attention: Sharding Along Sequence Dimension

ul.li: Attention scores alone: seq² × heads × 2 bytes
128K × 128K × 32 × 2 = 1 TB per layer! Solution: Shard sequence across GPUs

g.text: Seq[0:128K] | Full sequence on 1 GPU = OOM

ul.li: GPU 3: tokens [96K:128K] Memory per GPU: 1/P of total

div.p: Challenge: Each GPU needs to compute attention for ALL K,V pairs, but only has 1/P of the sequence. Ring Pattern:

ol.li: Each GPU computes local Q × local K
Rotate K,V blocks around the ring
After P steps, all pairs computed

ul.li: Attention scores: O(seq² / P) Communication per step:
Total: P × O(seq/P × hidden) = O(seq × hidden) Key insight: Total communication is independent of P (same as full sequence)
Full sequence would OOM on any GPU ✓ Linear memory scaling with P
Double GPUs → half memory per GPU ✓ Combines with Tensor Parallelism

div.p: 128K context configuration: TP=8, SP=8, PP=4, DP=4

g.text: def ring_attention(Q, K, V, ring_rank, ring_size):

div.p: Attention scores scale O(seq²) For 128K sequence:

ul.li: 8× reduction in local score memory SP is more effective than TP for long-context memory management.
